original_prompt: Refactor the booking modal in AppointmentsView to dynamically calculate the appointment's end time and visually reflect this on the calendar grid.
rephrased_goal: Implement real-time visual feedback for appointment duration in the booking modal. When a start time is selected or hardware duration changes, highlight the consumed time blocks on the grid and display the exact window (Start - End) in the header, with shift-end boundary checks.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Build time math helpers and header update logic
subtasks_required: 1.1 Create addMinutes(timeStr, mins) helper for HH:MM parsing and addition
1.3 Implement updateBookingWindowDisplay() to update #daily-view-date with Start-End window
1.4 Add basic boundary check logic (color text red if exceeding shift)
tasks.analogy_reference: Measure sun path before build wall
tasks.testing_criteria: Window text updates correctly in UI header when values change.

subtasks_required: 3.1 Add updateGridHighlighting() function to iterate through .calendar-cell
3.3 Apply .bg-primary .bg-opacity-25 (or similar) to consumed cells for the selected tech
3.4 Ensure old highlights are cleared on change
tasks.analogy_reference: Mark wood with charcoal before cut
tasks.testing_criteria: Selecting a cell highlights the following cells based on current duration.

subtasks_required: 4.1 Verify highlighting doesn't bleed into other technician columns
4.2 Verify highlighting updates when hardware (duration) changes
tasks.testing_criteria: Blocks align exactly with time slots.

subtasks_required: 5.1 Review view.appointments.js for any broken travel logic
5.2 Test edge case: appointment spanning past midnight (if applicable, though shifts usually don't)
tasks.analogy_reference: Walk whole mountain to see if cave safe
